methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $58M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series A), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Verdant Robotics, a Series A robotics and manufacturing company founded by Gabe Sibley, has raised $58M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of nine peers—one public and eight private—spanning the robotics sector at Series A and B stages. Notable comparables include 1X Technologies ($10.0B, Series B), Foundation Robotics ($3.0B, Series A), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B, Series B), and Dexterity ($1.6B, Series B), with peer valuations ranging from $300M to $10.0B.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression anchors to Verdant's $58M total raised and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yielding $116M. Peer-median methodology references the 7-peer cohort's median valuation of $1.6B, reflecting market pricing for same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies. The stage-progression method implies a lower bound, while peer-median suggests substantially higher market multiples for robotics at this maturity level.

Triangulation across both methods produces a valuation range of $99M (floor, derived from stage-progression adjustments) to $1.9B (ceiling, peer-median upper bound), with a median of $883M. The wide range reflects material uncertainty around which cohort provides the most representative benchmark—whether Verdant tracks with earlier-stage Foundation Robotics or later-stage peers like Bedrock and Dexterity. Product maturity, customer traction, and competitive positioning within robotics manufacturing remain key discriminators within this range.
